title: [Gov Healthcare] Corporate Partnership, Manager @Central (Business Development) #HCY
description:
Leadership & Partnership Management

  • Lead and motivate a team while managing strategic partnerships to drive productivity and performance

  • Foster a high-performance culture aligned with organisational values and objectives

  • Develop team priorities, set clear goals, and drive accountability against agreed targets

Insurance Partnership Management

  • Build and maintain strong relationships with insurance partners to drive demand creation initiatives

  • Demonstrate strong understanding of the insurance landscape, including unmet needs and latent demand

  • Plan, coordinate, and implement partnership initiatives such as CPA/LOG processes, hospital events, agent sharing, and partnership engagements

  • Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to resolve claim denials, disputes, and appeals, and liaise with insurers and clients for clarification

  • Work closely with senior leadership and key stakeholders to develop and execute strategies aligned with long-term growth objectives

Corporate Partnership Development

  • Build and manage strong relationships with corporate partners, translating partnerships into demand-driving initiatives

  • Drive demand creation through corporate partnerships in collaboration with internal stakeholders

  • Identify and leverage synergistic partnership opportunities to enhance awareness and engagement

  • Work with internal and external stakeholders to develop and refine services aligned with market trends

Operational & General Responsibilities

  • Develop and track relevant metrics to measure the effectiveness of initiatives and projects

  • Collate and analyse monthly data for management reporting

  • Review departmental operational procedures and work instructions regularly, implementing process improvements where required

  • Provide secretariat support for division, department, and workgroup meetings, including preparing minutes and following up on action items

  • Participate in organisational emergency planning exercises and provide support during emergencies or incidents when required

  • Perform any other duties as assigned

Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business Administration, Healthcare Management, or a related discipline

For interested candidates, please send your most recent and updated resume to sallytsin@recruitexpress.com.sg

WA me at http://wa.me/+6598326219 or tele me at @sallytsin

Tsin Chien Yee Sally
R25127272
Recruit Express Pte Ltd
EA License No. 99C4599

postedCompany.description:
Established since 1996, Recruit Express has emerged as the leading staffing solutions provider that has set a proven track record for matching the right people to the right organization within the shortest time. With our vast network of resources, extensive databases and defined recruitment processes, we have been successfully bridging talented job seekers of the highest calibre to employers who only want the best in their teams.


In our relentless pursuit of excellent service, we have adopted best practices and dynamic growth strategies in expanding our operations across the Asia Pacific regions: We have offices in Singapore, Sydney, Hong Kong, Kuala Lumpur, Taipei, Shanghai, Beijing, Tokyo and Bangkok.

With 400 permanent committed consultants from various professional backgrounds and disciplines, we make a difference by delivering top-notch services to our clients and candidates alike.
